2606.07441.title: Sycophantic Praise: Evaluating Excessive Praise in Language Models
2606.07441.paper_summary: Sycophancy in language models is typically studied as excessive agreement or validation, while explicit praise and flattery have received comparatively little attention. We argue that sycophantic praise is a distinct alignment problem that cannot be reliably measured using current methods. We introduce a parameterized framework that measures whether praise is excessive relative to contribution quality and expected user ability. We show that our framework substantially outperforms generic LLM judges in agreement with human annotations, and that sycophantic praise occurs far more frequently in social and interpretive domains than in objective reasoning settings. Together, these findings position praise calibration as a distinct alignment challenge.
2606.07441.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型中的諂媚通常被研究為過度的同意或驗證，而明確的讚美和奉承則相對較少受到關注。我們認為，諂媚的讚美是一個獨特的對齊問題，無法通過當前的方法可靠地測量。我們引入了一個參數化框架，用於測量讚美是否相對於貢獻質量和預期用戶能力過度。我們顯示，我們的框架在與人類標註的一致性方面顯著超越了通用的 LLM 評判者，而諂媚的讚美在社交和解釋性領域中發生的頻率遠高於在客觀推理環境中。總的來說，這些發現將讚美校準定位為一個獨特的對齊挑戰。
2606.07441.author: Daniel Vennemeyer et.al.
2606.07441.authors: Daniel Vennemeyer, Phan Anh Duong, Meryl Ye, Ruihong Huang, Tianyu Jiang
